top_stories.title: Microsoft Makes Passkeys Default: What Identity And Security Leaders Need To Do
top_stories.summary: Microsoft announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for all Microsoft Entra ID users starting September 1, 2026. The change will automatically enable passkey authentication, marking a significant shift in phishing-resistant MFA adoption across the platform.

top_stories.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
top_stories.summary: Identity-based attacks have surpassed exploits as the leading root cause of ransomware incidents. MFA was present in 97% of credential-based attacks yet failed to prevent compromise, highlighting gaps in authentication controls.

top_stories.title: Claude can now sign into websites with 1Password without exposing your credentials
top_stories.summary: 1Password has launched a beta integration called "1Password for Claude" that enables Anthropic's Claude AI assistant to authenticate to websites during browser tasks without exposing user credentials or secrets. The integration works across individual, family, and business 1Password plans with Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise subscribers on macOS using Claude Desktop and the 1Password browser extension.

top_stories.title: Oak raised $60M to give every user, machine, and AI agent one identity system
top_stories.summary: Oak, an Israeli identity startup, has emerged from stealth with a $60M seed round to build a unified identity system covering users, machines, and AI agents. The company is targeting the growing challenge of managing non-human identities alongside traditional user identities in enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
top_stories.summary: A token exchange vulnerability in n8n's workflow automation platform allowed attackers to log in as other users by exploiting improper JWT validation on Enterprise instances configured with multiple external token issuers. The flaw occurred because n8n matched incoming JWTs to local users using only the `sub` claim while ignoring the `iss` (issuer) claim, enabling cross-issuer account takeover without knowing the victim's password.

news.title: Microsoft warns of surge in ACR Stealer attacks on customers
news.summary: Microsoft has observed a surge in ACR Stealer malware attacks targeting enterprise customers, with the malware stealing browser-stored passwords, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents. The campaign directly targets authentication credentials and session tokens, posing risks to identity infrastructure and access management systems.

news.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
news.summary: Okta's Red Team discovered and reported a denial-of-service vulnerability in OpenSSL, dubbed "HollowByte," which allows an 11-byte TLS request to cause an unpatched server to reserve up to 131 KB of memory indefinitely. OpenSSL shipped the fix in June without a CVE, advisory, or changelog entry, prompting Okta to publish its own disclosure after finding the issue on glibc systems.

news.title: The script, not the voice, is what makes AI voice phishing work
news.summary: Researchers from Harvard Kennedy School, Meta, and other institutions conducted a study with 4,100 US adults testing AI voice phishing susceptibility using six commercial voice systems and human callers. The findings indicate that the persuasiveness of vishing attacks stems from the script content rather than voice quality, with the scenario involving a password reset request being a central test case.

news.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
news.summary: A Go-based botnet named NadMesh has been discovered actively scanning for exposed AI services to harvest cloud credentials, with the operator's own dashboard claiming 3,811 unique AWS keys collected. The botnet targets misconfigured AI tools including ComfyUI, Ollama, n8n, Open WebUI, Langflow, and Gradio to extract AWS keys and Kubernetes tokens.

news.title: ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
news.summary: ACR Stealer, an infostealer active since 2024, is stealing browser passwords, session tokens, and Microsoft 365 files including from OneDrive and SharePoint. The malware uses ClickFix social engineering techniques where users are tricked into manually executing malicious commands, bypassing traditional security controls to harvest authentication credentials and live session tokens.

news.title: Zoom Patches Critical Windows Flaw That Could Enable Account Takeover
news.summary: Zoom has patched a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-53412, CVSS 9.8) in Zoom Workplace for Windows that could enable account takeover. The flaw, caused by improper input validation, affects versions before 7.0.0 and multiple branches of the Zoom Workplace VDI Client for Windows.

news.title: Building Secure APIs with OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect
news.summary: The article covers API security implementation using OAuth 2.1 and OpenID Connect, addressing authentication and authorization flows, token management, PKCE, and security best practices. It serves as a technical guide for IAM professionals working with modern authentication protocols and API access control.

news.title: ESDS Launches Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, Two Sovereign Enterprise Security Platforms Built Entirely in India - The Tribune
news.summary: ESDS has launched two enterprise security platforms called Swaraj Nandi and Swaraj Hansa, described as sovereign, India-built solutions targeting privileged access management and security monitoring. Swaraj Nandi addresses privileged access governance while Swaraj Hansa focuses on security alert management and operations.

news.title: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
news.summary: Microsoft published guidance on applying least privilege principles to AI agents, covering identity, access controls, and tool binding. The article addresses how autonomous AI agents require strong identity, access management, and auditing controls to remain secure.

news.title: Keeper Security Closes the Standing Privilege Gap in Cloud Identity with Keeper Privileged Cloud
news.summary: Keeper Security launched Keeper Privileged Cloud, a new just-in-time access capability within its KeeperPAM platform. The feature grants privileged credentials only when needed and automatically revokes them when sessions expire, addressing standing privilege risks in cloud identity environments.

news.title: Microsoft introduces registry policy to automate Windows SSO consent
news.summary: Microsoft has introduced a registry-based policy control that allows IT administrators to automatically bypass SSO permission prompts on managed Windows 11 devices. The feature targets enterprise environments running specific Windows 11 versions, enabling automated SSO consent without user interaction.

news.title: Microsoft makes Windows SSO prompts easier to manage
news.summary: Microsoft is introducing a new registry-based policy allowing IT administrators to automatically accept Windows SSO permissions on Windows 11 versions 24H2 and 25H2 devices managed with Microsoft Entra ID. The policy applies to managed devices with work or school accounts, while personal Microsoft account users and unmanaged devices remain outside its scope.

news.title: Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
news.summary: CISA released a postmortem on a data breach in which a contractor exposed dozens of internal credentials, including AWS GovCloud keys, in a public GitHub repository for nearly six months. The incident highlights credential management failures and gaps in secrets detection practices relevant to IAM and privileged access management teams.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise enterprise systems.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly seizing high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force's Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions for the social engineering attack were distributed via Telegram, enabling unauthorized account takeovers by manipulating the AI chatbot into performing password resets.
